original_image_tags: Typed letter on Federal Bureau of Investigation stationery, addressed to Miss Laura McClaskey. The document features various administrative stamps including "RECORDED - 56" and "INDEXED - 50". There are handwritten notations in red and blue ink throughout, including case numbers and initials. The letter acknowledges receipt of data regarding a "Flying Saucer" sighting and states that the information has been referred to the Air Force. A distribution list of names is visible in the bottom left corner. The document shows signs of age with yellowing paper and punch holes at the top.

elements.description: Vertical ink stamp on the right side: 'RECEIVED REACTIC ROOM APR 11 7:19 PM '50 U.S. DEPT. OF JUSTICE F.B.I.'
Horizontal ink stamp at the bottom left: 'FILED 27 APR 27 1950'
Faint, illegible ink stamp in the bottom center.
A typed list of names along the bottom left edge, including Tolson, Ladd, Clegg, Glavin, Nichols, Rosen, Tracy, Harbo, Mohr, Tele. Room, Nease, and Gandy.

judge.reason: Description A describes a typed letter with administrative stamps and handwritten notations, while Description B provides specific, different quoted text for stamps and a typed list of names, creating direct contradictions in the visual content and specific text quoted.
